zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley於1354年出生在英國斯塔福爾海爾斯時，他的父親William le Boteler56歲，母親Joan Sudeley15歲。他於1385年7月18日之前與Alice de Beauchamp在英國瓦維其可爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1398年9月21日在英國史洛普爾維姆享年44歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley于1354年出生在英国斯塔福爾海爾斯时，他的父亲William le Boteler56岁，母亲Joan Sudeley15岁。他于1385年7月18日之前与Alice de Beauchamp在英国瓦維其可爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1398年9月21日在英国史洛普爾維姆享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley was born in 1354, in Hales, Staffordshire, England, his father, William le Boteler, was 56 and his mother, Joan Sudeley, was 15. He married Alice de Beauchamp before 18 July 1385, in Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 21 September 1398, in Wem, Shropshire, England, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley est né en 1354, à Hales, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, William le Boteler, avait 56 ans et sa mère, Joan Sudeley, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Alice de Beauchamp avant 18 juillet 1385, à Warwickshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 21 septembre 1398, à Wem, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley 1354 in Hales, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William le Boteler 56 und seine Mutter Joan Sudeley 15. Er hat vor 18. Juli 1385 in Warwickshire, England Alice de Beauchamp geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 21. September 1398 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Wem, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley è nato il nel 1354 nel Hales, Staffordshire, England, il suo padre, William le Boteler, aveva 56 anni e il suo madre, Joan Sudeley, aveva 15 anni. Lui sposato Alice de Beauchamp prima del 18 luglio 1385, nel Warwickshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 21 settembre 1398, nel Wem, Shropshire, England, all'età di 44 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeleyが1354年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアヘイレスで生まれた時，父William le Botelerは56歳，母Joan Sudeleyは15歳であった。1385年7月18日以前にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアでAlice de Beauchampと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1398年9月21日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアウエムで44歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley(이)가 1354년 영국 스태퍼드실르 헤일스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William le Boteler(은)는 56세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Sudeley(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1385년 7월 18일 이전 영국 워윅실르에서 Alice de Beauchamp(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1398년 9월 21일 영국 슈럽실르 베에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley nasceu em 1354, em Hales, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William le Boteler, tinha 56 anos e sua mãe, Joan Sudeley, tinha 15 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice de Beauchamp antes 18 de julho de 1385, em Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 21 de setembro de 1398, em Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 44 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley родился в 1354 г. в местности хэлесь, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William le Boteler было 56 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Sudeley, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice de Beauchamp перед 18 июля 1385 г. в местности варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 21 сентября 1398 г. в местности вем, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 44 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley nació en 1354, en Hales, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William le Boteler, tenía 56 años y su mamá, Joan Sudeley, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Alice de Beauchamp antes de 18 de julio de 1385, en Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 21 de septiembre de 1398, en Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 44 años.
